# Invariant

> Autonomous AI agents for compliance in mission-critical industries, space, aerospace, and nuclear.

Invariant builds autonomous AI agents that draft, file, and monitor regulatory and qualification compliance for companies in space, aerospace, and nuclear. The agents ingest design docs, test data, and the regulations that apply, then produce regulator-grade submissions, test plans, and verification/compliance matrices end to end, with every claim traced back to the rule that supports it. A small team of domain engineers handles deployment and high-stakes review. Backed by Entrepreneurs First.

## Key facts

- **State-of-the-art retrieval in nuclear:** Helion-512, our domain-adapted retrieval model, is the published state of the art on **FermiBench** (**0.9693 nDCG@10**), the only public retrieval benchmark for the nuclear regulatory domain.
- **Mission-critical industries served:**
  - **Space:** FAA 14 CFR Part 450 (launch + reentry), FCC Part 25 (satellite communications), NOAA Commercial Space Licensing (Earth-observation data), IN-SPACe NGP 2024 (Indian space authorisations), ITU radio coordination, ECSS standards, NASA GEVS (GSFC-STD-7000), MIL-STD-1540, MIL-STD-461 (EMI/EMC).
  - **Aerospace:** FAA 14 CFR Part 21/23/25 (type certification, airworthiness) for crewed and unmanned aviation systems.
  - **Nuclear:** US NRC 10 CFR Parts 50, 52, 53, 100, 110; IAEA SSR-2/1; for advanced reactor and SMR developers, including Safety Analysis Report (PSAR / FSAR) authoring and Request-for-Additional-Information (RAI) handling.
- **Founded** 2025. **Backed by** Entrepreneurs First.

## Pages

- [Home](https://invariant-ai.com/), Autonomous agents for mission-critical compliance.
- [Space compliance: a complete guide to global regulation, standards, and licensing](https://invariant-ai.com/space-compliance), A complete reference to space compliance: FAA Part 450, FCC Part 25, NOAA, IN-SPACe, ECSS, GEVS, ITU, qualification testing, and licensing timelines.
- [Nuclear compliance: the licensing pathways, what the regulator reads, and how AI changes the work](https://invariant-ai.com/nuclear-compliance), A complete guide to nuclear compliance for advanced reactor, SMR, and microreactor developers. Regulators, 10 CFR Parts 50/52/53, PSAR/FSAR drafting, and where AI agents help.

### Nuclear guides
- [Part 50 vs Part 52 vs Part 53: the three NRC reactor licensing pathways, explained](https://invariant-ai.com/part-50-vs-52-vs-53), A side-by-side comparison of 10 CFR Part 50, Part 52, and Part 53, history, structure, timelines, and which advanced reactor developers use each in 2026.
- [ITAAC closure under 10 CFR 52.99 and 52.103(g): the path to fuel load](https://invariant-ai.com/itaac-closure), How ITAAC closure works for a Part 52 combined license: ICN submission under 52.99(c)(1), Commission finding under 52.103(g), the 52.103(b) public hearing right, and Vogtle 3 and 4 precedent.
- [NRC RAI management: how to respond to Requests for Additional Information](https://invariant-ai.com/nrc-rai-management), A complete guide to NRC RAI process and response: LIC-115 Revision 1, 30-day window, RAI categories by SAR chapter, real-docket examples (NuScale, Kairos Hermes, Natrium), and the audit pivot under LIC-111.
- [10 CFR Part 53 subparts: a deep dive for advanced reactor builders](https://invariant-ai.com/part-53-subparts), Subpart-by-subpart reference for 10 CFR Part 53 covering Subparts A through H. 25 rem TEDE criterion, PRA scope, LBE selection, siting, FSAR, decommissioning, and the rule history.
- [How to write a Preliminary Safety Analysis Report (PSAR) for the NRC](https://invariant-ai.com/how-to-write-a-psar), A chapter-by-chapter drafting guide to the Preliminary Safety Analysis Report required for an NRC Construction Permit or Part 53 application.
- [How long does an NRC license application take?](https://invariant-ai.com/how-long-does-nrc-license-take), Realistic timelines for NRC license applications under Parts 50, 52, and 53, based on recent advanced-reactor dockets.
- [Advanced reactor licensing in the EU: ASNR, ONR GDA, and the SMR pre-licensing maze](https://invariant-ai.com/advanced-reactor-licensing-eu), A regional explainer comparing the UK GDA under ONR, France's ASNR review, and the patchwork of EU member-state regimes.
- [NRC pre-application engagement: the months that decide your application](https://invariant-ai.com/nrc-pre-application-engagement-guide), How pre-application engagement with the NRC actually works, and how it shapes the review timeline of a full application.
- [Nuclear compliance in India: AERB, the Atomic Energy Act, and private-sector access](https://invariant-ai.com/nuclear-compliance-india), India's nuclear regulatory architecture: AERB, DAE, and what is and is not open to private-sector participation in 2026.
- [Nuclear compliance in Japan: NRA, New Regulatory Requirements, restart](https://invariant-ai.com/nuclear-compliance-japan), Japan's post-Fukushima nuclear regulatory architecture: the NRA as an Article 3 commission, the 2013 New Regulatory Requirements, restart status, and the advanced reactor program.

### Space guides
- [How long does an FAA Part 450 launch license take?](https://invariant-ai.com/faa-part-450-license-timeline), A timeline-focused explainer that walks through the FAA Part 450 launch and reentry license process and the realistic wall-clock duration.
- [FAA Vehicle Operator License (VOL): the Part 450 single-license model](https://invariant-ai.com/faa-vehicle-operator-license), How a Part 450 VOL works: Section 450.3 scope, Section 450.5 issuance, Section 450.7 five-year term, the consolidation of legacy launch-specific and launch operator licenses, real VOLs issued since 2021.
- [ITU Bringing Into Use: the 7-year clock and the 90-day rule](https://invariant-ai.com/itu-bringing-into-use), A guide to the ITU Bringing Into Use mechanism under Article 11: the 7-year clock, the 90-day continuous transmission requirement, Resolution 35 NGSO milestones, and how the FCC mirrors the rule for US operators.
- [FCC Form 312 Schedule S: How to draft, file, and avoid rejection](https://invariant-ai.com/fcc-schedule-s), A drafting guide to FCC Form 312 Schedule S for satellite applications under 47 CFR 25.114: data blocks, GSO vs NGSO logic, antenna beams, orbital debris exhibits, the 2023 expedited rule, and rejection patterns.
- [How to file an ITAR Commodity Jurisdiction (CJ) request](https://invariant-ai.com/itar-commodity-jurisdiction), A drafting guide for the ITAR Commodity Jurisdiction (CJ) procedure under 22 CFR 120.12: Form DS-4076 content, the inter-agency State/Defense/Commerce review, typical timelines, and CJ Final Determination precedent for space hardware.
- [ITAR vs EAR for space companies: how to classify your hardware](https://invariant-ai.com/itar-vs-ear-for-space-companies), How USML Category XV (ITAR) and ECCN 9x515 (EAR) split jurisdiction over space hardware after the 2014 reform.
- [Space compliance in India: authorisations, licences, and foreign investment rules](https://invariant-ai.com/space-compliance-india), Everything a commercial space operator needs to clear before flying from or operating in India.
- [Space compliance in the EU: national licensing, ESA standards, and the cross-border stack](https://invariant-ai.com/space-compliance-eu), Why there is no single EU space regulator and how operators navigate the member-state patchwork.
- [The FCC 5-year deorbit rule explained](https://invariant-ai.com/fcc-5-year-deorbit-rule), FCC 22-74 compressed post-mission disposal for LEO satellites from 25 years to 5. Who is captured, what the order requires, and the design implications.
- [NOAA remote-sensing license tiers, explained](https://invariant-ai.com/noaa-remote-sensing-license-tiers), NOAA's three-tier commercial remote-sensing licensing framework and which tier your sensor falls into.
- [How to draft an ITU frequency-coordination filing](https://invariant-ai.com/how-to-draft-itu-coordination-filing), The ITU filing flow, the API and CR/C documents, and the interference-analysis content national administrations want.
- [ECSS vs MIL-STD: qualification testing standards for space](https://invariant-ai.com/ecss-vs-mil-std), A comparison of ECSS and MIL-STD families for space qualification testing, ECSS-E-ST-10-03C, MIL-STD-1540, SMC-S-016, MIL-STD-461G, NASA GEVS, and when to use each.
- [FAA Part 450 vs legacy launch licensing: the complete reference](https://invariant-ai.com/faa-part-450-vs-legacy), How 14 CFR Part 450 replaced legacy Parts 415, 417, 431, and 435 on March 10, 2021, the consolidation, the philosophical shift to performance-based regulation, and the transition.
- [FAA AC 450 series: complete advisory circular catalog for Part 450](https://invariant-ai.com/faa-ac-450-series), Every FAA Advisory Circular issued for 14 CFR Part 450, with section mapping, issue dates, and primary faa.gov sources. AC 413.13-1, 450.103-1, 450.115-1B, 450.139-1, 450.169-1A, and the rest.
- [How to write a Means of Compliance for FAA Part 450](https://invariant-ai.com/how-to-write-faa-part-450-means-of-compliance), The Means of Compliance is the single most important artefact in a Part 450 application. Here is how to write one.

### Product + research
- [Product](https://invariant-ai.com/product), What the agents produce: submissions, test plans, verification matrices.
- [Probe](https://invariant-ai.com/probe), Live semantic search across NRC ADAMS, powered by Helion-512.
- [Blog](https://invariant-ai.com/blog), Research, regulation comparisons, field notes.
- [Glossary](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary), Authoritative definitions of regulatory and qualification compliance terms.
- [Research](https://invariant-ai.com/research), Helion-512 retrieval model, FermiBench benchmark, published notes.
- [Compliance library](https://invariant-ai.com/compliance), Every Invariant resource for space, nuclear, and aerospace.
- [Trust](https://invariant-ai.com/trust), Security and data-handling disclosures.

### Calculators
- [FAA Part 450 timeline estimator](https://invariant-ai.com/calculators/faa-part-450-timeline), Interactive estimator for FAA 14 CFR Part 450 launch and reentry license duration.
- [NRC license timeline estimator](https://invariant-ai.com/calculators/nrc-license-timeline), Interactive estimator for NRC reactor license duration under Parts 50, 52, 53.

## Research

- [Invariant Sets State-of-the-Art on FermiBench](https://invariant-ai.com/blog/fermibench-sota), Helion-512 reaches 0.97 nDCG@10 on the only published nuclear-domain IR benchmark.
- [10 CFR Part 100 vs Part 53 Subpart D: A Siting Comparison](https://invariant-ai.com/blog/part100-vs-part53-siting), A line-by-line comparison of the legacy and the new technology-inclusive frameworks.
- [SSE/OBE → GMRS/SDC: The Seismic Design Shift to Part 53](https://invariant-ai.com/blog/seismic-design-shift), The deterministic two-tier framework is replaced by risk-tiered ground motions and seismic design categories.

## Glossary entries (DefinedTerm)

Space:
- [14 CFR Part 413](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/14-cfr-413), FAA Office of Commercial Space Transportation regulation in Title 14, Chapter III, Subchapter C that sets the cross-cutting license application procedures for all U.S. commercial launch, reentry, and site licenses.
- [ANSI S2.20-1983](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ansi-s2-20), Acoustical Society of America standard for estimating air blast characteristics from single-point explosions, used by FAA AC 450.137-1 as the screening glass damage model for window breakage probability.
- [BIS Commerce Country Chart](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/bis-country-chart), A destination-by-control-reason matrix in Supplement No. 1 to 15 CFR Part 738 that exporters cross-reference against an ECCN to determine whether an EAR license is required.
- [COMSTAC](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/comstac), The Commercial Space Transportation Advisory Committee is a FACA-chartered federal advisory body that advises the Secretary of Transportation, through the FAA, on commercial space transportation policy and regulation.
- [Commercial Space Launch Amendments Act of 2024](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/space-launch-safety-act), Informal label for the 2024 statutory amendments to U.S. commercial space launch law, enacted across P.L. 118-63 and P.L. 118-159, that reset FAA AST authorities and extended the human-spaceflight learning period.
- [ECCN 9A521](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ec-cn-9a521), A common but inexact shorthand for the Commerce Control List entries covering specially designed spacecraft components transferred from the USML, properly classified at ECCN 9A515.x under 15 CFR Part 774.
- [ECSS](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ecss), ECSS is the European Cooperation for Space Standardization, a coherent single set of standards for space project management, engineering, and product assurance maintained by ESA, industry, and national agencies.
- [ECSS-E-ST-10-03](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ecss-e-st-10-03), ECSS-E-ST-10-03C Rev.1 (31 May 2022) is the European space engineering standard defining ground verification testing requirements for spacecraft segment elements and equipment before launch.
- [FAA AC 450.41-1](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ac-450-41-1), FAA Advisory Circular 450.41-1 describes the process for submitting and obtaining acceptance of a means of compliance under 14 CFR 450.35 for commercial launch and reentry licensing.
- [FAA AST](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-ast), The FAA Office of Commercial Space Transportation, the U.S. agency that licenses commercial launch and reentry under 51 USC Chapter 509 and 14 CFR Parts 400 to 460.
- [FAA Categorical Exclusion](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-categorical-exclusion), A NEPA determination under FAA Order 1050.1F that a proposed action, including a 14 CFR Part 450 launch or reentry license, normally has no significant environmental effect and needs no EA or EIS.
- [FAA Part 450](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-part-450), FAA Part 450 is the performance-based 14 CFR rule that consolidates commercial launch and reentry licensing into a single, vehicle-agnostic framework, effective March 10, 2021.
- [FAA SLR-2](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/slr-2), FAA rulemaking effort to revise 14 CFR Part 450, launched in response to the August 2025 SpARC final report and Executive Order 14335 of August 13, 2025.
- [FAA Spaceport License](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/spaceport-license), An FAA Launch Site Operator License issued under 14 CFR Part 420 that authorizes a commercial spaceport operator to offer a launch site to multiple launch and reentry vehicle operators.
- [FCC 22-74 (5-year deorbit)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/fcc-22-74), FCC 22-74 is the Second Report and Order adopted September 29, 2022 that shortened the post-mission disposal benchmark for LEO satellites from 25 years to 5 years.
- [FCC Part 25](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/fcc-part-25), 47 CFR Part 25 is the FCC rule set that licenses commercial satellite communications, covering U.S. space stations, earth stations, and non-U.S. market access requests.
- [FCC Part 5](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/fcc-part-5), 47 CFR Part 5 is the FCC's Experimental Radio Service rule, which authorizes non-commercial experimentation, product development, and market trials, including space and earth station experimental authorizations.
- [FCC Part 97](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/fcc-part-97), FCC 47 CFR Part 97 governs the U.S. Amateur Radio Service, including amateur satellites operated under noncommercial rules, with space station requirements set in Section 97.207.
- [FCC Schedule S](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/fcc-schedule-s), FCC Form 312 Schedule S is the structured satellite engineering data form required for Part 25 space station applications, capturing beam, frequency, EIRP, G/T, and orbit parameters.
- [FCC market access](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/fcc-market-access), The FCC process under 47 CFR 25.137 by which a non-U.S. licensed satellite system obtains authority to provide service to or from earth stations located in the United States.
- [Flight Safety System (FSS)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/flight-safety-system), The high-reliability onboard plus ground system, required under 14 CFR 450.145, that terminates or aborts a launch or reentry vehicle whose failure would endanger the public.
- [GIMS GXT file format](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/gxt-format), ITU-defined textual file format that encodes satellite antenna gain contour diagrams, used in FCC Schedule S exhibit S8 to satisfy 47 CFR 25.114 antenna pattern submission requirements.
- [IADC Space Debris Mitigation Guidelines](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/iadc), The international consensus framework (IADC-02-01) for limiting orbital debris, structured around protected LEO and GEO regions, post-mission disposal, and on-orbit passivation across all spaceflight phases.
- [IADC and UN COPUOS debris guidelines](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/imo-debris), Voluntary international space debris mitigation guidelines, drafted by IADC in 2002 and adopted by UN COPUOS in 2007, codifying disposal practice for LEO and GEO operators.
- [IN-SPACe NGP 2024](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/in-space-ngp-2024), IN-SPACe NGP 2024 is the May 2024 norms document operationalising the Indian Space Policy 2023 by setting authorisation procedures for six categories of space activities by Indian entities.
- [ISO 24113](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/iso-24113), ISO 24113:2023 is the top-level international standard defining space debris mitigation requirements for unmanned spacecraft and launch vehicle orbital stages operating in or transiting near-Earth space.
- [ITU API and CR/C filings](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/itu-api-crc), ITU Radio Regulations procedure under Articles 9 and 11 in which an operator files Advance Publication Information then a Coordination Request, securing a frequency assignment within a 7-year deadline.
- [ITU Resolution 49 (Rev. WRC-19)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/resolution-49), ITU procedure requiring an administration to file identity data on the satellite network operator, spacecraft manufacturer, and launch services provider within the seven-year bringing-into-use period.
- [Kessler syndrome](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/kessler-syndrome), A self-sustaining collisional cascade in low Earth orbit in which fragment-generating impacts raise debris density until further collisions become inevitable, rendering affected orbital regimes unusable.
- [Liability Convention 1972](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/liability-convention), The 1972 UN treaty establishing absolute liability of launching states for surface and aircraft damage caused by space objects, and fault-based liability for damage in space.
- [License Exception CSA](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/license-exception-csa), License Exception Commercial Space Activities (CSA) is a Bureau of Industry and Security authorization, proposed in October 2024, allowing license-free exports of specified space items to close allies and U.S. persons.
- [MIL-STD-461](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/mil-std-461), MIL-STD-461 is the U.S. Department of Defense interface standard governing electromagnetic interference emission and susceptibility characteristics of electronic subsystems and equipment.
- [NASA GEVS](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nasa-gevs), NASA Goddard's General Environmental Verification Standard (GSFC-STD-7000), the agency baseline that defines test levels and methods for qualifying spacecraft hardware to mission environments.
- [NOAA Commercial Remote Sensing Licensing](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/noaa-csla), A NOAA licensing regime under 15 CFR Part 960 that authorizes private Earth remote sensing space systems and sorts them into three tiers based on data availability.
- [NOAA Tiers 1, 2, 3](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/noaa-tier-1-2-3), A three-tier U.S. commercial remote-sensing licensing framework under 15 CFR Part 960 (effective July 20, 2020) that scales license conditions based on whether comparable unenhanced data is available from foreign or domestic sources.
- [Thermal vacuum (TVAC)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/thermal-vacuum-tvac), Thermal vacuum (TVAC) is an environmental test that exposes spacecraft hardware to high vacuum and cyclic hot and cold extremes to verify thermal design, workmanship, and functional performance.
- [U.S. Government ODMSP](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/odmsp), The U.S. Government Orbital Debris Mitigation Standard Practices, first issued in 2001 and revised November 2019, set the baseline debris rules for NASA, FCC, NOAA, and FAA licensees.
- [UN Outer Space Treaty](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/unoosa-ost), The 1967 multilateral treaty governing State activity in outer space, establishing non-appropriation, State responsibility for national activities, launching-State liability, and a duty to avoid harmful contamination.
- [USML Category IV](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/usml-cat-iv), USML Category IV controls launch vehicles, guided and ballistic missiles, rockets, torpedoes, bombs, mines, and their specially designed parts under U.S. State Department ITAR jurisdiction.
- [USML Category XV](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/usml-category-xv), USML Category XV is the ITAR control list at 22 CFR 121.1 covering spacecraft and related articles whose export is licensed by the State Department's DDTC.

Nuclear:
- [10 CFR 2.815 (acceptance review)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nrcpr), NRC regulation governing docketing and acceptance review of standard design certification applications, requiring a completeness and technical adequacy determination generally within 30 days of tender.
- [10 CFR Part 100](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/10-cfr-part-100), U.S. NRC regulation establishing reactor site suitability criteria, including exclusion area, low population zone, population center distance, and geologic and seismic siting requirements for power and testing reactors.
- [10 CFR Part 110](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/10-cfr-part-110), Nuclear Regulatory Commission regulation governing export and import of nuclear equipment, material, and related technology, prescribing general and specific license procedures under the Atomic Energy Act.
- [10 CFR Part 50](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/10-cfr-part-50), 10 CFR Part 50 is the NRC's legacy two-step reactor licensing rule, issuing a construction permit and then a separate operating license for production and utilization facilities.
- [10 CFR Part 52](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/10-cfr-part-52), The U.S. NRC regulation, codified in 1989, that authorises a one-step Combined Licence for nuclear power plants alongside Early Site Permits and Standard Design Certifications.
- [10 CFR Part 53](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/10-cfr-part-53), 10 CFR Part 53 is the NRC's risk-informed, technology-inclusive, performance-based licensing framework for commercial nuclear plants, offered as an optional alternative to Parts 50 and 52.
- [10 CFR Part 71](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/10-cfr-part-71), U.S. NRC regulation governing packaging and transportation of radioactive material, setting Type A and Type B package design, testing, and quality assurance standards for licensed shipments.
- [10 CFR Part 73](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/10-cfr-part-73), NRC regulation titled "Physical Protection of Plants and Materials" that establishes security requirements for nuclear facilities, special nuclear material, and the Design Basis Threat licensees must defend against.
- [ALARA](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/alara), "As low as is reasonably achievable," the NRC radiation protection principle in 10 CFR 20.1003 requiring licensees to drive doses below regulatory limits using practical, economically justified measures.
- [ARCAP](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/arcap), ARCAP is the U.S. NRC project that produced technology-inclusive, risk-informed guidance covering the non-safety-analysis content of advanced reactor applications under 10 CFR Parts 50, 52, and 53.
- [ARDP](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ardp), U.S. Department of Energy program launched in 2020 to cost-share two full-scale advanced reactor demonstrations, TerraPower's Natrium and X-energy's Xe-100, within seven years.
- [ASME Section III](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/asme-section-iii), ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code Section III sets construction rules for nuclear facility components, classifying them as Class 1, 2, or 3 by safety significance.
- [ASME/ANS RA-S](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/asme-ans-ra-s), Joint ASME and ANS family of consensus PRA standards establishing technical requirements for Level 1 and Level 2 probabilistic risk assessments at U.S. commercial nuclear power plants.
- [ASNR (France)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/asnr), France's independent civil nuclear regulator, the Autorité de sûreté nucléaire et de radioprotection, formed on 1 January 2025 by merging ASN and IRSN.
- [Atomic Energy Act of 1954](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/aea-1954), The foundational US statute (P.L. 83-703, 42 USC 2011 et seq.) that opened nuclear technology to private industry and established the federal civilian licensing framework.
- [CNSC (Canada)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/cnsc), Canada's independent federal nuclear regulator, established May 31, 2000 under the Nuclear Safety and Control Act, overseeing reactors, the fuel cycle, uranium mining, and nuclear substances.
- [Combined License (COL)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/combined-license), A Combined License issued under 10 CFR 52 Subpart C is a single NRC authorization to both construct and operate a nuclear power facility, contingent on satisfying ITAAC.
- [Early Site Permit (ESP)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/early-site-permit), A U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission approval under 10 CFR 52 Subpart A that resolves site safety, environmental, and emergency planning issues for one or more future nuclear power reactors, independent of design.
- [Energy Reorganization Act of 1974](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/era-1974), Federal statute (Pub. L. 93-438, 88 Stat. 1233) that abolished the Atomic Energy Commission and split its functions between a new Nuclear Regulatory Commission and the Energy Research and Development Administration.
- [IAEA Convention on Nuclear Safety](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/cns), A 1994 international treaty, administered by the IAEA and in force since 1996, that commits Contracting Parties to safety obligations for land-based civil nuclear power plants enforced through three-yearly peer review.
- [IAEA INFCIRC/153](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/infcirc-153), IAEA INFCIRC/153 (Corrected), issued June 1972, is the model Comprehensive Safeguards Agreement that every non-nuclear-weapon State party to the NPT must conclude under Article III.
- [IAEA Nuclear Activities Authorisation](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/iaea-naa), Composite IAEA framework under which States authorise peaceful nuclear activities, combining INFCIRC/153 safeguards obligations with GSR Part 1 (Rev. 1) regulatory authorisation requirements for facilities and activities.
- [IAEA SF-1](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/iaea-sf-1), IAEA Safety Standards Series No. SF-1 (2006), the Safety Fundamentals document that states the fundamental safety objective and ten principles underpinning all subsequent IAEA nuclear and radiation safety requirements.
- [IAEA SSR-2/1](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/iaea-ssr-2-1), IAEA Specific Safety Requirements No. SSR-2/1 establishes design requirements for civilian land-based stationary nuclear power plants, currently in Revision 1 published in 2016.
- [ITAAC](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/itaac), ITAAC are the inspections, tests, analyses, and acceptance criteria written into a 10 CFR Part 52 combined license that the NRC must find complete before fuel load.
- [ITAAC Post-Closure Notification](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ipcn), A licensee filing under 10 CFR 52.99(c)(2) reporting new information that materially alters the basis for a previously submitted ITAAC closure, made before the 52.103(g) finding.
- [KBS-3](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/kbs-3), KBS-3 is the Swedish multi-barrier concept for final disposal of spent nuclear fuel in copper canisters within bentonite clay roughly 500 metres deep in crystalline bedrock.
- [Kairos Hermes](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/kairos-hermes), Kairos Power's 35 MWt fluoride-salt-cooled high-temperature test reactor at Oak Ridge, the first non-light-water construction permit issued by the NRC in over 50 years.
- [LIC-500](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/lic-500), LIC-500 is the U.S. NRC Office of Nuclear Reactor Regulation office instruction governing the topical report process, most recently issued as Revision 9 in January 2022.
- [NEI 18-04](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nei-18-04), Nuclear Energy Institute guidance, Revision 1 (August 2019), defining the Licensing Modernization Project methodology for non-light-water reactor licensing basis development, endorsed by NRC Regulatory Guide 1.233.
- [NEI 21-04](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nei-21-04), Nuclear Energy Institute industry guidance, issued in 2021, prescribing a standardized format and best practices for applicant responses to NRC Requests for Additional Information (RAIs).
- [NEI 21-07 (TICAP)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nei-21-07-ticap), NEI 21-07 Revision 1 is the Technology Inclusive Content of Application Project guideline, endorsed by NRC Regulatory Guide 1.253 in April 2024, defining a risk-informed, performance-based safety analysis report format for non-LWR license applications.
- [NEIMA](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/neima), NEIMA is the Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act, Public Law 115-439 (January 14, 2019), directing the NRC to build a technology-inclusive regulatory framework and reform fee recovery for advanced reactors.
- [NEPA Environmental Assessment](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nepa-ea), A concise NEPA screening document, capped at 75 pages and a one-year deadline, that determines whether a federal action requires a full Environmental Impact Statement or a Finding of No Significant Impact.
- [NEPA review](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nepa), NRC environmental review under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969, producing an Environmental Impact Statement that informs nuclear reactor licensing decisions under 10 CFR Part 51.
- [NRA (Japan)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nra-japan), Japan's Nuclear Regulation Authority, the independent civilian regulator established 19 September 2012 under the Ministry of the Environment to oversee all civil nuclear safety and security.
- [NRC Certificate of Compliance](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nrc-coc), An NRC-issued approval listing a specific dry spent fuel storage cask design at 10 CFR 72.214, authorizing power reactor licensees to load it under a Part 72 general license.
- [NRC DANU](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/danu), DANU is the NRC Office of Nuclear Reactor Regulation division that since 2022 has led pre-application engagement and licensing review for advanced reactors and non-power facilities.
- [NRC Emergency Planning Zone](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/epz), Geographic area around a U.S. commercial nuclear reactor for which detailed offsite emergency planning is required under 10 CFR 50.47, sized to accident consequences.
- [NRC Inspection Manual](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/inspection-manual), The NRC's public compilation of Inspection Manual Chapters (IMCs) and Inspection Procedures (IPs) that govern how staff inspect, document, and assess licensee activities.
- [NRC LIC-111](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/lic-111), NRC NRR Office Instruction LIC-111, Revision 2 (issued December 30, 2024, ML24309A281), the staff guidance governing planning, conduct, and reporting of regulatory audits.
- [NRC LIC-115](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/lic-115), NRR Office Instruction governing how NRC staff prepare, clarify, and issue Requests for Additional Information to applicants, with a 30-day default response window and tiered acceptance versus detailed review RAI tracks.
- [NRC LIC-117](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/lic-117), NRC Office Instruction governing the acceptance review of new reactor license applications, verifying completeness and technical sufficiency before docketing for detailed technical review.
- [NRC RG 1.183](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-1-183), U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide issued July 2000 establishing the alternative source term (AST) methodology and assumptions for analyzing design basis accident radiological consequences at light-water reactors.
- [NRC RG 1.200](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-1-200), U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide 1.200 endorses, with conditions, the ASME/ANS PRA Standard as an acceptable approach for risk-informed licensing activities at commercial nuclear plants.
- [NRC RG 1.232](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-1-232), NRC Regulatory Guide 1.232 Revision 0, issued April 2018, provides advanced reactor design criteria adapting 10 CFR Part 50 Appendix A GDC for non-light-water reactor principal design criteria.
- [NRC RG 1.247](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-1-247), Trial NRC regulatory guide, issued March 2022, that endorses with exceptions ASME/ANS RA-S-1.4-2021 as an acceptable PRA standard for advanced non-light water reactor risk-informed activities.
- [NRC RG 4.7](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-4-7), The U.S. NRC Regulatory Guide that defines general site suitability criteria for commercial nuclear power stations, including population density, geology, hydrology, meteorology, and emergency planning factors.
- [NRC SECY paper](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nrc-secy), A SECY paper is a written issue paper that NRC staff submit through the Office of the Secretary to the five Commissioners, recommending a policy, rulemaking, licensing, or adjudicatory decision.
- [NRC Staff Requirements Memorandum](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/srm), A Staff Requirements Memorandum (SRM) is the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's written direction to staff, recording Commission decisions on a SECY paper plus any tasks and due dates.
- [NUREG-0800 (SRP)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nureg-0800), NRC Standard Review Plan, LWR Edition: chapter-by-chapter guidance that NRC staff use to review safety analysis reports for nuclear power plant licensing.
- [NuScale VOYGR](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nuscale-voygr), NuScale's small modular reactor plant family, built from 77 MWe NuScale Power Modules; the original 50 MWe variant was certified by the NRC in 2023 under 10 CFR Part 52 Appendix G.
- [Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/npt), The 1968 multilateral treaty, in force since 5 March 1970, that binds 191 states to three pillars: non-proliferation, peaceful nuclear cooperation, and pursuit of nuclear disarmament.
- [ONR Generic Design Assessment](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/onr-gda), A voluntary four-step UK regulatory review by the Office for Nuclear Regulation and Environment Agency that assesses a new reactor design generically before any site-specific licensing.
- [Oklo Aurora](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/oklo-aurora), A 1.5 MWe sodium-cooled fast microreactor whose custom combined license application became the first advanced-reactor COLA denied without prejudice by the NRC on January 6, 2022.
- [PSAR / FSAR](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/psar-fsar), The PSAR and FSAR are the two-stage Safety Analysis Reports an applicant submits to the U.S. NRC under 10 CFR 50.34 to obtain a construction permit and operating license.
- [RAI (Request for Additional Information)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rai-nrc), A Request for Additional Information is the formal NRC docket mechanism by which staff ask a license applicant to supply or clarify information needed to complete a regulatory finding.
- [Regulatory Guide 1.206](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-1-206), Regulatory Guide 1.206 Revision 1 (October 2018) is the NRC's consolidated guidance on the format and content of 10 CFR Part 52 applications for design certifications, early site permits, and combined licenses.
- [Regulatory Guide 1.215](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-1-215), NRC Regulatory Guide 1.215 describes a method the staff finds acceptable for documenting closure of inspections, tests, analyses, and acceptance criteria under 10 CFR Part 52.
- [Regulatory Guide 1.233](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/rg-1-233), NRC guidance, issued June 2020 as Revision 0, that endorses NEI 18-04 as one acceptable technology-inclusive, risk-informed, performance-based methodology for non-light-water reactor licensing.
- [SECY-23-0021](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/nrc-secy-23-0021), NRC staff paper dated March 1, 2023, transmitting the draft proposed 10 CFR Part 53 rule, a risk-informed, technology-inclusive licensing framework for commercial nuclear plants.
- [Section 123 Agreement](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/123-agreement), A bilateral peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement concluded under Section 123 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954, required before the United States may license significant exports of nuclear material, equipment, or sensitive technology.

Aerospace:
- [Bilateral Aviation Safety Agreement](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/baa), A government-to-government treaty enabling the FAA and a counterpart civil aviation authority to reciprocally recognize each other's airworthiness approvals, type certificates, and production approvals.
- [DO-160](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/do-160), RTCA DO-160G (2010) is the standard defining environmental conditions and test procedures, from temperature and vibration to HIRF and lightning, for certifying airborne electronic equipment.
- [DO-178C](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/do-178c), DO-178C, "Software Considerations in Airborne Systems and Equipment Certification," is the 2011 RTCA/EUROCAE standard certification authorities accept as the means of compliance for airborne software.
- [DO-254](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/do-254), DO-254 is the RTCA design assurance standard for airborne electronic hardware, published April 2000 and endorsed by the FAA as an acceptable means of compliance for certification.
- [DO-330](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/do-330), RTCA DO-330 / EUROCAE ED-215, the 2011 Software Tool Qualification Considerations document supplementing DO-178C, defining five Tool Qualification Levels and objectives for qualifying development and verification tools.
- [Design Assurance Level (DAL A-E)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/dal-a-e), A five-tier civil-aviation rigor scale (A through E) tied to failure-condition severity, dictating how exhaustively airborne systems, hardware, and software must be developed and verified.
- [EASA](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/easa), EASA is the European Union agency responsible for civil aviation safety, established in 2002 to issue type certificates and harmonize airworthiness rules across EU Member States.
- [EASA CS-25](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/easa-cs-25), EASA Certification Specifications CS-25 are the European airworthiness code for turbine-powered large aeroplanes above 5,700 kg MTOW, the structural counterpart to FAA Part 25.
- [FAA 14 CFR Part 21](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-part-21), The FAA regulation in Title 14 of the Code of Federal Regulations that prescribes procedures for certificating civil aircraft, engines, propellers, and articles in the United States.
- [FAA 14 CFR Part 23](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-part-23), FAA airworthiness standards for normal category airplanes up to 19 seats, restructured in 2017 around performance-based rules and industry consensus standards as accepted means of compliance.
- [FAA 14 CFR Part 25](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-part-25), Federal airworthiness standards governing the type certification of transport category airplanes, the design rulebook for jets and large turboprops operated in U.S. civil aviation.
- [FAA 14 CFR Part 27](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-part-27), FAA airworthiness standard governing type certification of normal category rotorcraft with maximum weight of 7,000 pounds or less and nine or fewer passenger seats.
- [FAA 14 CFR Part 29](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-part-29), The FAA airworthiness code for transport category rotorcraft, prescribing type-certification standards for large helicopters above 7,000 lb or with 10 or more passenger seats.
- [FAA 14 CFR Part 33](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/faa-part-33), FAA airworthiness standards governing the type certification of aircraft engines, covering design, construction, and block-test requirements for both reciprocating and turbine powerplants.
- [FAA AC 25.1309-1](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ac-25-1309), FAA Advisory Circular describing acceptable means of compliance with 14 CFR 25.1309 system safety requirements for transport-category airplanes; current Revision B issued August 30, 2024.
- [FAA AC 25.1309-1B](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/ac-25-1309-1b), FAA Advisory Circular providing acceptable means of compliance with 14 CFR 25.1309 for transport aircraft system safety, defining five failure-condition severities and matching probability targets per flight hour.
- [FAA Special Conditions](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/special-conditions), Design-specific airworthiness regulations the FAA issues under 14 CFR 21.16 when existing standards lack adequate or appropriate safety requirements for a novel or unusual feature.
- [FAA TSO](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/tso), An FAA Technical Standard Order is a minimum performance standard issued for specified articles used on civil aircraft, with authorization granted under 14 CFR Part 21 Subpart O.
- [ICAO](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/icao), ICAO is the United Nations specialized agency that codifies international civil aviation Standards and Recommended Practices through 19 Annexes to the 1944 Chicago Convention.
- [ICAO Annex 14](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/icao-annex-14), ICAO Annex 14 sets the international Standards and Recommended Practices for aerodromes, covering runway geometry, obstacle limitation surfaces, visual aids, and rescue and firefighting services.
- [ICAO Annex 8](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/icao-annex-8), Annex 8 to the Chicago Convention sets Standards and Recommended Practices for the airworthiness of aircraft, defining the minimum design and continuing-airworthiness basis Contracting States must recognize.
- [JARUS SORA](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/sora), A risk-based, ten-step methodology issued by JARUS for authorising uncrewed aircraft operations in the Specific category, calibrating evidence and design assurance to assessed ground and air risk.
- [MIL-STD-810](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/mil-std-810), The U.S. Department of Defense environmental engineering and laboratory test method standard that tailors temperature, vibration, shock, humidity, and altitude testing to a materiel system's life cycle.
- [MIL-STD-882E](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/mil-std-882e), U.S. Department of Defense Standard Practice for System Safety, issued 11 May 2012, defining the systems-engineering approach for identifying hazards and managing risk across defense systems.
- [Modular Open Systems Approach (MOSA)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/mosa), MOSA is the US DoD statutory acquisition strategy, codified at 10 USC 4401, that mandates modular design and consensus-based open interfaces for major defense programs to enable competition and refresh.
- [SAE ARP4754A](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/arp-4754a), SAE Aerospace Recommended Practice ARP4754A, dated December 21, 2010, sets the development assurance process for civil aircraft and their systems used in certification.
- [SAE ARP4754B](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/arp-4754b), SAE Aerospace Recommended Practice (Dec 20, 2023) for civil aircraft and systems development, jointly issued as EUROCAE ED-79B, aligned with ARP4761A safety assessment.
- [SAE ARP4761](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/arp-4761), SAE Aerospace Recommended Practice that codifies the safety assessment process for civil airborne systems, defining FHA, PSSA, SSA, and CCA methods to demonstrate compliance with 14 CFR 25.1309.
- [Type Certificate (TC)](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/type-certificate), An FAA design approval issued under 14 CFR 21 Subpart B that certifies an aircraft, engine, or propeller type design meets the applicable airworthiness and environmental requirements.
- [eVTOL certification](https://invariant-ai.com/glossary/evtol), The regulatory pathway by which electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft earn FAA type, production, and operating approval under the new powered-lift category established in 2024.

## Contact

- founders@invariant-ai.com
- Backed by [Entrepreneurs First](https://www.joinef.com)
